channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Florida’s move from 2000s vexation to 2020s role model a blueprint for Arizona, lawmakers say
item.description: Arizona Republicans hope to make their state's election processes flow as smoothly as Florida's, noting how that state too had its share of snafus and issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The Trump administration has blocked The Associated Press from traveling with President Trump on Air Force One and also from covering an Oval Office event. It's part of an ongoing terminology dispute over the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Planned Parenthood appears to scrub Instagram as fears of DOGE cuts loom
item.description: Planned Parenthood seemed to scrub all content from its Instagram shortly after HHS Secretary RFK Jr.'s swearing in without an explanation. Some believe the organization fears DOGE cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 most likely GOP successors to Trump in 2028
item.description: President Trump raised eyebrows in an interview that aired Monday after he declined to name Vice President Vance as his automatic successor ahead of the 2028 presidential election. “No, but he is very capable,” Trump told fox News’ Bret Baier when asked if he viewed Vance as his 2028 successor.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's mugshot spotted hanging in ornate frame near Oval Office
item.description: Social media posts showed a framed front page 2023 mugshot of President Trump from the New York Post near the Oval Office in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New poll reveals growing number of Dems want party to move in new direction
item.description: After the Democratic Party's setbacks to Trump and Republicans in the 2024 elections, a new national poll indicates Democrats increasingly want their party to move toward the center.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's reciprocal tariffs will overturn decades of trade policy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is taking a blowtorch to the rules that have governed world trade for decades. The “reciprocal’’ tariffs that he announced Thursday are likely to create chaos for global businesses and conflict with America’s allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PIX on Politics Panel breaks down Adams-DOJ controversy
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a politically charged week for Mayor Eric Adams after the Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to drop a federal corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: EEOC seeks to drop transgender discrimination cases, citing Trump's executive order
item.description: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission has moved to dismiss six of its own cases on behalf of workers alleging gender identity discrimination, arguing that the cases now conflict with President Donald Trump’s recent executive order.
item.dc:creator: Claire Savage And Alexandra Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls for withholding federal money from schools and colleges that require COVID vaccines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools, colleges and states that require students to be immunized against COVID-19 may be at risk of losing federal money under a White House order signed Friday by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Vance Eviscerates Europe
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state AGs accuse Vance of spreading 'dangerous lie' following VP's online criticism of judges
item.description: Democratic attorneys general slammed Vice President JD Vance for attempting "to spread this dangerous lie" after he criticized judges blocking President Donald Trump's agenda in the courts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance meets with leader of far-right German party
item.description: Vice President Vance met Friday with a leader of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party, making him the highest-ranking U.S. official to do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith announces she won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith (D) will not run for reelection in 2026, opening up a seat in the Democratic-leaning state, she announced Thursday. Smith said in a video posted to the social platform X that she has “loved” her job as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: The art of the deal? Zelenskyy says a Ukraine-Russia agreement must come through Trump negotiations
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy appealed to President Donald Trump's history as a dealmaker on Friday, offering flattering words as he sought the U.S. president's leadership in negotiations that could end the Russia-Ukraine conflict.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, MONIKA SCISLOWSKA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell tests the strengths and limits of his power opposing a trio of Trump's Cabinet nominees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No longer in charge, Sen. Mitch McConnell has been speaking his mind, the long-serving GOP leader rejecting President Donald Trump’s more high-profile Cabinet nominees — alone at times, among the Republicans, casting his no votes.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Calls for Mayor Adams’ resignation mount amid turmoil at DOJ
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Calls for Mayor Eric Adams’ resignation are mounting after the Department of Justice ordered his case to be dropped. One call came from Lt. Gov. Antonio Delgado, second-in-command to Gov. Kathy Hochul. Hochul is the only person with the power to remove Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Annexing Canada? At Niagara Falls, Trump's idea gets eye rolls but not support
item.description: NIAGARA FALLS, N.Y. (AP) — At Niagara Falls, the iconic honeymoon and tourist destination on the U.S.-Canadian border, visitors from both countries took in President Donald Trump’s unsolicited suggestion that the U.S. annex its neighbor to the north with an air of detached amusement.
item.dc:creator: MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Guarded optimism in India as Trump and Modi outline plans to deepen defense partnership
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — There was guarded optimism among military experts in India as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump outlined plans to ramp up sales of defense systems to New Delhi, including F-35 stealth fighter jets, to deepen the U.S.-India strategic relationship.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An open Minnesota Senate seat is enticing to plenty but unclear how many will take plunge
item.description: Minnesota has its first open-seat Senate race since 2006. Many candidates are giving it a look and both parties say they’re well-positioned to win.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Can suspending a cage-free egg law solve the soaring price problem? Nevada takes a crack at it
item.description: Record high egg prices prompted Nevada lawmakers to unanimously back suspending their cage-free egg law, but the six other states with similar laws in effect have shown no sign of dropping them even if it might help lower grocery bills.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Sejal Govindarao And Ty Oneil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Order to drop New York Mayor Adams' case roils Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams — a stunning escalation in a dayslong standoff over the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Concerns over mass deportations lead to ‘Know Your Rights‘ discussion in Brooklyn Center
item.description: Amid concerns over the Trump Administration’s plans for mass deportations, nearly three dozen people who live in Brooklyn Park and Brooklyn Center attended a panel discussion Wednesday night to ask questions about how to respond if ICE shows up at a home or workplace, and also asked about the role o
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: What Sen. Tina Smith’s retirement means for Minnesota politics in 2026
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith announced she wouldn’t seek reelection on Thursday, opening a big field on both sides to replace her in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Brian Bakst, Alanna Elder, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. confirmed as Trump’s health secretary, over Democrats’ loud objections
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. wins confirmation to be President Trump’s secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services. The vote was 52 to 48.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: Minnesotans, other politicians react to Sen. Tina Smith's decision not to run again
item.description: Minnesota politicians from both sides of the aisle have weighed in on the news that U.S. Sen. Tina Smith, who has served in the Senate since 2018, is retiring after next year.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Watch live: House panel convenes hearing on USAID amid DOGE overhaul
item.description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee is holding a hearing Thursday morning looking into what GOP leadership has called the "betrayal" of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID).
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
